VAL-COMP-001.evidence: grep for 'diff_scenes' in compositor/main.rs returns zero matches. diff_scenes exists only in libraries/scene/lib.rs (definition) and test/tests/scene.rs (test usage). The compositor render loop uses dr.change_list() and dr.is_full_repaint() instead.
VAL-COMP-001.details: The render loop (main.rs lines 469-535) implements change-list-driven damage: (1) node count change → full repaint, (2) is_full_repaint() sentinel → full repaint, (3) change_list() empty → skip, (4) change_list() non-empty → per-node dirty rects. No reference to diff_scenes anywhere in the compositor.

VAL-COMP-002.evidence: Code inspection of main.rs lines 483-527 shows change list node IDs translated to dirty rects via scene::abs_bounds() for new positions and PREV_BOUNDS static for old positions. Both old and new position rects are added to the DamageTracker.
VAL-COMP-002.details: For each node_id in the change list: (a) old position read from PREV_BOUNDS[node_id] → damage.add(), (b) new position computed via scene::build_parent_map + scene::abs_bounds → damage.add(). Positions scaled to physical pixels via scale_factor. PREV_BOUNDS populated by populate_prev_bounds() after full repaints (line 104) and updated per-node after partial updates (lines 575-593).

VAL-COMP-003.evidence: Code inspection of main.rs lines 470-472: 'if curr_count != prev_node_count { damage.mark_full_screen(); }'. This is the first check in the damage logic chain, before change list inspection.
VAL-COMP-003.details: prev_node_count initialized to 0 (line 426, ensuring first frame is always full repaint), updated at end of each frame (line 600: prev_node_count = curr_count). When node count differs (e.g., selection rects added/removed), full-screen repaint is triggered immediately.

VAL-COMP-005.evidence: Code inspection of scene_render.rs lines 303-328: child iteration computes child bounding box as ClipRect, checks child_clip.intersect(child_rect).is_some() before calling render_node recursively. Children outside the clip are skipped entirely (no recursion into subtree).
VAL-COMP-005.details: The clip rect is passed down through the recursion. For each child: cx/cy/cw/ch computed from child_node position × scale, formed into a ClipRect, intersection tested. Only children with non-empty intersection call render_node. Comment explicitly states: 'This avoids visiting entire subtrees (and their glyph cache lookups) when the dirty region covers only a small portion of the screen.'

VAL-COMP-006.evidence: Code inspection of main.rs lines 478-482: 'Some(changed) if changed.is_empty() => { prev_node_count = curr_count; continue; }'. The continue skips all rendering and GPU signaling.
VAL-COMP-006.details: When change_list() returns Some with an empty slice, the `continue` statement skips: all render logic (render_scene/render_scene_clipped), PresentPayload construction, gpu_ch.send(), and sys::channel_signal(GPU_HANDLE). Only prev_node_count is updated before continuing to wait for the next scene update.

VAL-COMP-007.evidence: Code inspection of main.rs lines 473-475: 'else if dr.is_full_repaint() { damage.mark_full_screen(); }'. This is checked AFTER the node count check (line 470) and BEFORE the change list inspection (line 477), making it distinct from VAL-COMP-003.
VAL-COMP-007.details: is_full_repaint() checks hdr.change_count == FULL_REPAINT (u16::MAX). The FULL_REPAINT sentinel is set by SceneWriter::mark_changed() when change_count >= CHANGE_LIST_CAPACITY (24 entries), and by SceneWriter::clear() for full rebuilds. The if/else if chain ensures: VAL-COMP-003 handles node count changes, VAL-COMP-007 handles overflow with unchanged node count — mutually exclusive code paths.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 6 passed, 0 failed. All compositor optimization assertions verified through code inspection — change-list-driven damage tracking replaces diff_scenes, dirty rects derived from node geometry, full repaint on structural changes and overflow, subtree clip skipping in render_node, empty change list skips rendering entirely.
